# SysKnife Contributor License Agreement

> **Note:** SysKnife was relicensed from AGPL-3.0-only to MIT in April 2026.
> No CLA is required for new contributions — they are accepted under the MIT
> License and the Developer Certificate of Origin (<https://developercertificate.org>).
> This file is retained as a historical record of the original CLA terms that
> governed contributions before the relicensing.

---

## Original CLA (superseded — for historical reference only)

By submitting a pull request to the SysKnife project
(`github.com/lacs-project/sysknife`) you agreed to the following terms.

### 1. Definitions

- **"You"** means the individual or legal entity submitting a contribution.
- **"Contribution"** means any original work of authorship submitted to the project.
- **"Project"** means the SysKnife software and all associated repositories under
  the `lacs-project` GitHub organisation.

### 2. Copyright licence

You granted LACS Foundation and its successors a perpetual, worldwide,
non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable licence to reproduce, prepare derivative
works of, publicly display, publicly perform, sublicense, and distribute your
Contribution and such derivative works in source or object form under any licence,
including the MIT License and any future open-source licence the project adopts.

### 3. Patent licence

You granted LACS Foundation a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free,
irrevocable patent licence to make, use, sell, offer for sale, import, and
otherwise transfer your Contribution where such licence applies only to those
patent claims licensable by you that are necessarily infringed by your Contribution
alone or in combination with the Project.

### 4. Representations

You represented that:

- You were legally entitled to grant the above licences.
- If your employer had rights to intellectual property you create, you received
  permission to make the Contribution on behalf of that employer, or your employer
  waived such rights.
- Your Contribution was your original creation and did not include material subject
  to third-party licences incompatible with MIT.

### 5. No warranty

Your Contribution was provided "as is" without warranties of any kind.
